data.body:
## Description

**What**: Add a new _phase_version_bump to merge-to-main.sh that calls bump-version.sh after pulling latest main. Accept a --bump flag (patch default, minor when --bump minor is passed). Remove COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 3b (patch version bump) entirely.
**Why**: Eliminates guaranteed merge conflicts on plugin.json when parallel worktrees merge. Since merges are serialized by the existing lock file, the bump always reads the latest version from main.
**Scope**:
- IN: New _phase_version_bump in merge-to-main.sh, --bump flag parsing, state file integration for --resume, removing Step 3b from COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md, handling version.file_path absent (skip silently)
- OUT: Sprint skill changes (separate story), documentation updates (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, merge-to-main.sh includes a _phase_version_bump that reads the current version from main after pull, increments it via bump-version.sh, and folds the result into the merge commit (via amend) before pushing
  <- Satisfies: merge-to-main.sh accepts a --bump flag; the version bump phase reads the current version from main after pull, increments it, commits the result, and continues to the push phase
- When this story is complete, merge-to-main.sh accepts a --bump flag (patch by default, minor when --bump minor is passed)
  <- Satisfies: merge-to-main.sh accepts a --bump flag (patch default, minor for epic completion)
- When this story is complete, when version.file_path is absent in dso-config.conf, the version bump phase is skipped silently with no error
  <- Satisfies: when version.file_path is absent, the bump phase is skipped silently with no error
- When this story is complete, COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 3b (patch version bump) no longer exists — no version-related logic remains in the commit workflow
  <- Satisfies: COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 3b (patch version bump) is removed entirely
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] New phase must integrate with merge-to-main.sh --resume state file — if interrupted mid-bump, resume must handle it
- [Reliability] bump-version.sh failure during merge must not leave main in a half-pushed state — fail before push
- [Testing] merge-to-main.sh test suite needs coverage for new phase and --bump flag
- [Maintainability] --bump flag convention must be consistent with existing merge-to-main.sh argument parsing
- [Reliability] Version bump commit strategy must fold into the merge commit (via amend, like _phase_validate does) rather than creating a separate commit — a separate commit would trigger the Layer 1 review gate requiring review-status + diff hash
- [Reliability] Folding into the merge commit via amend keeps PRE_MERGE_SHA (used by _phase_ci_trigger) valid — a separate commit would require updating _phase_ci_trigger diff logic
